content: Comparative performance differences between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and dedicated curious agents can be used as a performance benchmark to identify which intrinsic reward signals optimally bootstrap 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agents with structured episodic memory, reducing the sample complexity of open-ended environment exploration. The resulting curious active learning agent can prioritize memorizing only high-uncertainty, high-novelty state-action pairs that align with its intrinsic learning goals, rather than storing all encountered experiences.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
